message:
feat(types+connectors): add NormalizedInvocation for structured tool/skill extraction

Why: Previously, tool and skill usage within agent sessions was only
accessible by parsing free-text content strings or digging into the
opaque `extra` JSON blob on each NormalizedMessage. This made it
impossible to programmatically answer questions like "which tools did
this session use?" or "how many skill invocations happened?" without
connector-specific parsing at every call site.

This commit introduces a first-class `NormalizedInvocation` struct and
populates it across all 18 connectors, enabling structured queries over
tool/skill usage without downstream consumers needing to understand each
connector's raw format.

What changed:

1. New type: `NormalizedInvocation` (src/types.rs)
   - Fields: kind ("tool" | "skill"), name (canonical searchable name),
     raw_name (original wrapper name when unwrapped), call_id (provider
     correlation ID), arguments (raw input payload).
   - Serialization: default+skip_serializing_if on optional fields to
     keep JSON output compact when invocations are absent.

2. Shared extraction helpers (src/connectors/utils.rs)
   - `extract_invocations_from_content_blocks()`: Parses Claude API-style
     content block arrays (type: "tool_use") into NormalizedInvocations.
     Works for any connector that stores content as typed block arrays
     (amp, claude_code, codex, cline, factory).
   - `unwrap_skill_invocations()`: Amp-specific post-processor that
     detects wrapper tools ("skill", "load_skill") and rewrites them to
     kind="skill" with the inner semantic name, preserving the original
     wrapper name in raw_name for traceability.
   - 11 comprehensive unit tests covering: plain tool_use extraction,
     multiple tools, missing names, null/string inputs, skill wrapper
     unwrapping for both "skill" and "load_skill", non-wrapper passthrough,
     and missing inner-name edge cases.

3. Connector-by-connector population of `invocations` field:
   - Active extraction (parse tool_use blocks from content):
     amp, claude_code, cline, codex, factory — use the shared
     `extract_invocations_from_content_blocks` helper.
   - Custom extraction (connector-specific tool call formats):
     kimi — extracts from payload.name/toolName with input/arguments/
     parameters fallback chain.
     openclaw — extracts from content array blocks with type="toolCall".
     pi_agent — extracts from content array blocks with type="toolCall".
   - Amp additionally calls `unwrap_skill_invocations` to resolve
     skill("github-prs") wrappers to their semantic names.
   - Empty invocations (Vec::new()) for connectors whose session formats
     don't expose structured tool calls: aider, chatgpt, clawdbot,
     copilot, copilot_cli, crush, cursor, gemini, opencode, qwen, vibe.

4. Public API surface (src/lib.rs, src/connectors/mod.rs)
   - Re-exports NormalizedInvocation, extract_invocations_from_content_blocks,
     and unwrap_skill_invocations so downstream crates can use them.

5. Test fixture updates (src/connectors/gemini.rs)
   - All existing NormalizedMessage test literals updated to include the
     new `invocations: vec![]` field to satisfy the struct's exhaustive
     construction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -22,6 +22,7 @@ pub use types::{
     // Scan & provenance types
     LOCAL_SOURCE_ID,
     NormalizedConversation,
+    NormalizedInvocation,
     NormalizedMessage,
     NormalizedSnippet,
     Origin,
@@ -48,7 +49,8 @@ pub use connectors::{
     cline::ClineConnector, codex::CodexConnector, copilot::CopilotConnector,
     copilot_cli::CopilotCliConnector, estimate_tokens_from_content, extract_claude_code_tokens,
     extract_codex_tokens, extract_tokens_for_agent, factory::FactoryConnector, file_modified_since,
-    flatten_content, franken_detection_for_connector, gemini::GeminiConnector,
+    extract_invocations_from_content_blocks, flatten_content,
+    franken_detection_for_connector, gemini::GeminiConnector,
     get_connector_factories, kimi::KimiConnector, normalize_model, openclaw::OpenClawConnector,
     parse_timestamp, pi_agent::PiAgentConnector, qwen::QwenConnector, token_extraction,
     vibe::VibeConnector,

@@ -50,6 +50,31 @@ pub struct NormalizedMessage {
     pub content: String,
     pub extra: serde_json::Value,
     pub snippets: Vec<NormalizedSnippet>,
+    /// Structured tool/skill invocations extracted from this message.
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
+    pub invocations: Vec<NormalizedInvocation>,
+}
+
+/// A single tool or skill invocation within a message.
+#[cfg(feature = "connectors")]
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
+pub struct NormalizedInvocation {
+    /// Classification: `"tool"` or `"skill"`.
+    pub kind: String,
+    /// Canonical searchable name (e.g. `"github-prs"`, `"Read"`, `"bash"`).
+    /// For wrapper tools like Amp's `skill("github-prs")`, this is the
+    /// unwrapped semantic name, not `"skill"`.
+    pub name: String,
+    /// Original tool name when different from `name` (e.g. `"skill"` for
+    /// Amp skill wrapper calls).
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    pub raw_name: Option<String>,
+    /// Provider-assigned call ID, useful for joining with tool results.
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    pub call_id: Option<String>,
+    /// Raw input/arguments passed to the tool.
+    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    pub arguments: Option<serde_json::Value>,
 }
 
 #[cfg(feature = "connectors")]
